generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (const, eq) use valid types: int, real, or bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts reside within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations (e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`).
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing the contract itself.
Do not use implementation-level details (subcomponent paths) in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, owners)
